asset_name: UI atlas — title, pause, clear, and game-over overlays
shared_properties: All front-end screens use one atlas format and remain centered in screen space.
The warm cream text treatment stays readable against the sepia world palette.
Instruction anchors remain consistent so player input prompts are easy to find on every overlay.
differences: This atlas packages the title, pause, stage-clear, and game-over screen layouts into one resource.
The clear panel uses celebratory gold emphasis while the game-over panel uses deeper red pressure.
The title panel leaves room for the game name and a start prompt while the pause panel prioritizes concise resume and quit guidance.

context.usage: Overlay atlas for title, pause, stage clear, and game-over screens that keep session flow readable.

shape.silhouette: Four full-screen layout panels for title, pause, clear, and game-over messaging.

acceptance_criteria: Sprite sheet is exactly 5120x720px with 4 cells of 1280x720px and 0px spacing.
Each cell preserves a readable central title area plus lower instruction anchors for Space, Esc, Q, and R prompts.
The game-over cell uses a stronger wine-red emphasis than the title or clear cells so failure reads instantly.

reference_images.label: leaf_beat_gdd
reference_images.purpose: Grounds the core loop, timing windows, obstacle pacing, UI layout, and Bevy target constraints.

reference_images.label: leaf_beat_moodboard
reference_images.purpose: Grounds the sepia autumn atmosphere, tree silhouettes, and warm color layering.

bevy_constraints.bundle_strategy: TextureAtlas-driven overlay entity set for the front-end and between-state screens.
bevy_constraints.asset_loading: Load ui/ui_frontend_overlay_states_5120x720.png during UI boot and switch atlas cells according to the active screen state.
runtime_notes: Keep each overlay cell centered at the full 1280x720 frame while leaving room for score and high-score text widgets.
Use one atlas so transitions between title, pause, clear, and game-over states avoid extra texture churn.
Expose state-to-cell mapping in UI resources so input handlers can switch screens without filename lookup.
